titleStmt.title: Translation of BL Cotton Ch. xviii.16

div.p: William, by the grace of God king of the Scots, to all worthy men of the whole of his land, clerk and lay, greeting. Let present and future know me to have given, conceded, and by this my charter to have made firm, to God, the church of St Mary at Melrose, and to the monks serving God in that place, one full toft in my burgh of Lanark, to be held in free, pure, and everlasting alms, as freely, quitly, fully, and honourably as they freely, quitly, fully and honourably hold and possess their other alms. As witness: Hugh my chancellor; Philip de Valognes the chamberlain; Richard de Prebenda; William de la Haye; William Gifford; Duncan de Lascelles. At Perth on the first day of June.
